content.text:
Hey, 
Hand-over — DataQ, end of 2026-07-05 session
State of play
Cycle: v1.1 Week 2 (Portability — due 2026-07-18). Prior weeks W1 done; v1.0.0 shipped 2026-07-04.
Repo: main @ 4df1e71c, clean. No open PRs. Prod is deployed at main HEAD (api/worker/frontend on 4df1e71c).
Open issues: 64. Harness: fully asleep (all 4 Airflow apps Stopped, both ADF triggers Stopped, all 6 jobs — mockdata ×5 + dbt-lineage — runningStatus: Suspended). Verified this session.
What shipped this session (all merged + deployed + live-verified)
The alerting bucket for W2 is complete:

#656 — #386 (dedup severity single-source) + #387 (suppression guard on operationally-failed runs) + #388 (alert_on drift-guard). #389 closed won't-fix.
#657 — #655: one canonical db.models.SEVERITY_RANK + FAILING_TIERS + shared worst_severity() helper (converged 3 duplicated rank maps + 2 worst-severity loops).
#659 — #416: enriched Slack/email alerts (deep link, run metadata, expected-vs-observed) via a new shared pure alerting/render.py.
#662 — #661: fully tabular email (run-details + failing-checks tables) + owner name across Slack/email/Teams (Teams card brought to #416 parity: metadata facts + "View run" action).
Key operational learnings (saved to memory)
Alerting IS configured + delivering in prod — Slack (channel-slack-webhook) + Gmail email (theurgicduke771@gmail.com, channel-email-password) + PUBLIC_BASE_URL. Teams is implemented but a no-op (no TEAMS_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME set).
Why "no notifications" happened: dedup suppresses repeat/unchanged failures. To live-test, create a NEW suite (new id → not deduped) with a guaranteed-fail check (expect_table_row_count_to_be_between min 1e9 on Snowflake — Retail (DATAQ_READER) / RETAIL.ORDERS_HEADER, observed ~24960).
Headless prod API: az account get-access-token --resource api://21491115-d620-4fe8-9c66-8d567dfae601 → Bearer vs https://dataq-app-frontend.…/api/v1 (api is internal; frontend proxies). Deploy is manual: gh workflow run Deploy --ref main.
CI gotcha: #573 (SchedulesPanel Popconfirm) is a known flake — re-run the failed frontend job if it trips.
Watch-outs / known live issues
Snowflake — Orders/Payments-backed suites (Orders Header, Payments, Customers, Products) fail on every prod run in ~3s with checks_total: 0 and no reason — masked by #605 (no failure reason surfaced) + #571 (checks_total 0). Root cause (likely creds/connectivity on those two connections) is undiagnosable until #605 ships. Snowflake — Retail (DATAQ_READER) suites run fine.
Subscription clock: Snowflake + Azure both end ~2026-07-25. W3 is the deliberate wind-down (#590/#591). Front-load anything needing live cloud.
Suggested next steps (remaining W2, pick up any)
#532 — dry-run preview: extend Snowflake-only → UC + flat-file.
#584 / #488 — MCP NL tool-selection spot-check + workspace-admin visibility in MCP/schedules (need live /mcp before the Azure wind-down).
UI-gap fixes user flagged: #617 (mobile/responsive — likely highest user impact), #618 (stale MSAL label), #652 (dbt missing from Triggers UI), #651 (check-authoring validation), #653 (snooze UI), #654 (connection history UI), #647 (dbt mislabeled as airflow).
Correctness/UX: #605 (surface run failure reasons — unblocks the Snowflake-Orders diagnosis), #571, #643.
Everything is committed, deployed, verified, and the harness is confirmed paused. Nothing left dangling. 


Can you pickup the UI related items
I'll pick up the UI-gap issues from the hand-over (#617, #618, #647, #651, #652, #653, #654). First, let me read each issue to understand scope and plan the order of attack.

input.command: gh issue view 617 --json number,title,body,labels,milestone -q '{n: .number, t: .title, m: .milestone.title, labels: [.labels[].name], body: .body}' && echo ---- && gh issue view 618 --json number,title,body,labels,milestone -q '{n: .number, t: .title, m: .milestone.title, labels: [.labels[].name], body: .body}'

{"body":"## Summary

The app is effectively unusable on a mobile / narrow viewport. Several layouts were built for desktop widths only and never collapse, stack, or provide horizontal scroll. Originally reported from a phone (screenshots): tables have no scrollbar and columns are cut off, and the **suite detail** page renders its title/description **one character per line**.

I swept every top-level screen with Playwright at a **390×844** viewport (iPhone-class, dev-bypass auth, seeded demo data). The reported issues reproduce and there are more of the same family. Grouped below by root cause, most severe first.

Tested routes: `/dashboard`, `/connections`, `/suites`, `/suites/:id`, `/results` (Runs + Pipeline runs), `/results/:runId`, `/profile`, `/settings`, `/admin`, `/suites/:id/checks/new` (+ form).

---

### Bug 1 — Suite detail two-panel layout never stacks → title/description wrap one char per line (**critical**, the primary report)

**Repro:** open any suite (`/suites/:id`) at ≤ ~430px wide.

**Observed:** the list panel + detail panel stay side-by-side. The detail pane is squeezed to a ~20px sliver on the far right, so the suite title and description render vertically (one character per line), largely off-screen. The per-suite notification section (`Send alerts for this suite`, `Teams webhook`) does the same.

**Measured (Playwright `getBoundingClientRect`):** the detail `<h4>` title is **60px wide × 224px tall at left=369px** (viewport is 390px). Other detail-pane spans measure 9–12px wide × 264–462px tall.

**Root cause:** [frontend/src/pages/Suites.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Suites.tsx#L216-L243) — the selected-suite view is `<Flex gap={24} align="flex-start">` with a fixed-width list `<Card style={{ width: 320, flexShrink: 0 }}>` and a `<div style={{ flex: 1, minWidth: 0 }}>` detail pane. At 390px minus 48px content padding (~342px usable), the 320px card + 24px gap leave ≈ 0px for `flex:1, minWidth:0`, which collapses to near-zero and forces per-character wrapping. There is no breakpoint that switches this row to a vertical stack (or hides one pane) on narrow screens.

---

### Bug 2 — AntD tables have no horizontal scroll on mobile → columns clipped, no scrollbar (**high**, the reported table issue)

**Repro:** `/results` (Runs tab and Pipeline runs tab), `/results/:runId`, `/admin`, dashboard recent-runs.

**Observed:** tables are wider than the viewport but the wrapper clips them (`overflow-x: visible`) instead of scrolling. Rightmost columns are cut off with **no scrollbar** and no way to reach them:
- Results → **Runs**: `Trigger` column cut off. Table width **676px** in a 341px wrapper (`overflow-x: visible`, `wrapScrolls: false`).
- Results → **Pipeline runs**: `Env` / provider-run columns cut off.
- **Run detail** (`/results/:runId`): per-check results table `Metric` column cut off.
- **Admin**: a **564px** table clipped at 390px.
- **Dashboard**: a ~580px table clipped inside its card.

**Root cause:** none of the `<Table>` usages set `scroll={{ x }}` (grep for `scroll={{` in `frontend/src` returns **zero** hits). AntD only makes a table horizontally scrollable when given `scroll={{ x: <min-width|'max-content'> }}`; without it the table overflows and the ancestor card clips it. Affected: [pages/Results.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx), [pages/RunDetail.tsx](frontend/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx), [pages/Admin.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx), [components/dashboard/RecentRuns.tsx](frontend/src/components/dashboard/RecentRuns.tsx), [components/suites/SchedulesPanel.tsx](frontend/src/components/suites/SchedulesPanel.tsx), [components/checks/ColumnProfilePanel.tsx](frontend/src/components/checks/ColumnProfilePanel.tsx).

**Suggested fix:** add `scroll={{ x: 'max-content' }}` (or an explicit min width) to these tables.

---

### Bug 3 — Zero-width Sider trigger (☰) floats over page headings on every screen (**medium**)

**Repro:** every route at ≤ `lg` (the Sider auto-collapses via `breakpoint="lg"`).

**Observed:** the collapsed-Sider trigger (☰) floats at the top-left of the content area and overlaps the page `<h1>` / tabs on nearly every page:
- Connections title → "Connecti…" with ☰ over it
- Run detail title → leading "P" of "PERF baseline…" hidden behind ☰
- Settings → ☰ over the "General" tab
- Check form → "…alues not null" (leading "v" hidden)
- Dashboard subtitle partly covered

**Root cause:** [frontend/src/App.tsx](frontend/src/App.tsx#L120-L157) — `<Sider breakpoint="lg" collapsedWidth={0}>` renders AntD's default zero-width trigger, which is absolutely positioned at the top edge of the content and isn't accounted for by page padding, so it sits on top of each page's heading.

**Suggested fix:** move the toggle into the `<Header>`, or set a `zeroWidthTriggerStyle` that clears the heading, or reserve top padding when collapsed.

---

### Bug 4 — Page header (title + action buttons) doesn't wrap/stack on mobile → title squeezed (**medium**)

**Repro:** `/connections` (clearest), also contributes to Bug 3's overlap on other pages.

**Observed:** the header is a single non-wrapping row of `[title | action buttons]`. On Connections the title is compressed to "Connecti / ns" and collides with the `Test all` / `Add connection` buttons.

**Root cause:** per-page header `<Flex justify="space-between">` rows (e.g. [pages/Connections.tsx](frontend/src/pages/Connections.tsx)) with no `wrap`/stacked-on-narrow behavior.

**Suggested fix:** allow the header Flex to wrap (`wrap` / stack vertically) below a breakpoint.

---

### Not affected (render fine at 390px)
- `/connections` list cards, `/suites` list cards, `/profile`, the check-editor wizard steps and the check form (single-column, stacks correctly — aside from Bug 3's ☰ overlap on its title).

### Notes
- Playwright screenshots captured locally during the sweep (dashboard, connections, suites, suite-detail, results runs/pipeline, run-detail, profile, settings, check-editor). Happy to attach if useful.
- Scope is CSS/layout responsiveness only; no data or auth issues involved.
- There is currently no responsive/mobile coverage in the Playwright e2e suite (all specs run `Desktop Chrome`) — a mobile-viewport project could guard against regressions once these are fixed.


---

## Screenshots (Playwright, 390×844)

![suite detail](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/assets/issue-617-mobile/docs/assets/issue-617/m-04-suite-detail.png)

{"body":"## Summary

The Profile and Workspace Settings pages hardcode the authentication method as **"Azure AD (MSAL)"**, but MSAL was retired in favor of a generic OIDC client (`oidc-client-ts`) during the cloud-neutral cutover — see [ADR 0028](docs/adr/0028-cloud-neutral-image-runtime-config-generic-oidc.md) ("**replace MSAL with a generic OIDC client**"; MSAL retired). The label is now both **inaccurate** (no MSAL) and **off-message** for the provider-neutral auth posture (ADR 0010 — Azure is one impl behind the `AUTH_*` seam, not the architecture).

## Locations
- [frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx:61](frontend/src/pages/Profile.tsx#L61) — `<Descriptions.Item label="Authentication">Azure AD (MSAL)</Descriptions.Item>`
- [frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx:67](frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx#L67) — same hardcoded string

## Observed
Both pages render **Authentication: Azure AD (MSAL)** (seen at runtime on the deployed app and locally).

## Suggested fix
Drop the "(MSAL)" qualifier. Either a provider-neutral label (e.g. "OIDC (SSO)") or derive it from the runtime `DATAQ_AUTH_*` config rather than hardcoding a provider/library name in two places. Keep it consistent across both pages.

## Notes
Spotted while sweeping screens for the mobile-UI issue (#617); filing separately since it's a content/staleness bug, not a layout one.","labels":["bug"],"m":"v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth","n":618,"t":"Profile & Settings show stale "Azure AD (MSAL)" auth label after MSAL→generic-OIDC cutover (ADR 0028)"}

content.text:
Review target: `679`

`high effort → 3+5 angles × 6 candidates → 1-vote verify (recall-biased) → ≤10 findings`

You are reviewing for **recall** at high effort: catch every real bug a careful
reviewer would catch in one sitting. At this level, catching real bugs matters
more than avoiding false positives. Err on the side of surfacing.

## Phase 0 — Gather the diff

Run `git diff @{upstream}...HEAD` (or `git diff main...HEAD` / `git diff HEAD~1`
if there's no upstream) to get the unified diff under review. If there are
uncommitted changes, or the range diff is empty, also run `git diff HEAD` and
include the working-tree changes in scope — the review often runs before the
commit. If a PR number, branch name, or file path was passed as an argument,
review that target instead. Treat this diff as the review scope.

## Phase 1 — Find candidates (3 correctness angles + 3 cleanup angles + 1 altitude angle + 1 conventions angle, up to 6 each)

Run **8 independent finder angles** via the Agent tool. Each
surfaces **up to 6 candidate findings** with `file`, `line`, a one-line
`summary`, and a concrete `failure_scenario`.

### Angle A — line-by-line diff scan

Read every hunk in the diff, line by line. Then Read the enclosing function for
each hunk — bugs in unchanged lines of a touched function are in scope (the PR
re-exposes or fails to fix them). For every line ask: what input, state, timing,
or platform makes this line wrong? Look for inverted/wrong conditions,
off-by-one, null/undefined deref, missing `await`, falsy-zero checks,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in catch, unescaped regex metachars.

### Angle B — removed-behavior auditor

For every line the diff DELETES or replaces, name the invariant or behavior it
enforced, then search the new code for where that invariant is re-established.
If you can't find it, that's a candidate: a removed guard, a dropped error
path, a narrowed validation, a deleted test that was covering a real case.

### Angle C — cross-file tracer

For each function the diff changes, find its callers (Grep for the symbol) and
check whether the change breaks any call site: a new precondition, a changed
return shape, a new exception, a timing/ordering dependency. Also check callees:
does a parallel change in the same PR make a call unsafe?

### Reuse

The angles above hunt for bugs; this one and the next two hunt for cleanup in
the changed code. Flag new code that re-implements something the codebase
already has — Grep shared/utility modules and files adjacent to the change,
and name the existing helper to call instead.

### Simplification

Flag unnecessary complexity the diff adds: redundant or derivable state,
copy-paste with slight variation, deep nesting, dead code left behind. Name
the simpler form that does the same job.

### Efficiency

Flag wasted work the diff introduces: redundant computation or repeated I/O,
independent operations run sequentially, blocking work added to startup or
hot paths. Also flag long-lived objects built from closures or captured
environments — they keep the entire enclosing scope alive for the object's
lifetime (a memory leak when that scope holds large values); prefer a
class/struct that copies only the fields it needs. Name the cheaper
alternative.

### Altitude

Check that each change is implemented at the right depth, not as a fragile
bandaid. Special cases layered on shared infrastructure are a sign the fix
isn't deep enough — prefer generalizing the underlying mechanism over adding
special cases.

### Conventions (CLAUDE.md)

Find the CLAUDE.md files that govern the changed code: the user-level
~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, the repo-root CLAUDE.md, plus any CLAUDE.md or
CLAUDE.local.md in a directory that is an ancestor of a changed file (a
directory's CLAUDE.md only applies to files at or below it). Read each one
that exists, then check the diff for clear violations of the rules they state.

Only flag a violation when you can quote the exact rule and the exact line
that breaks it — no style preferences, no vague "spirit of the doc"
inferences. In the finding, name the CLAUDE.md path and quote the rule so the
report can cite it. If no CLAUDE.md applies, return nothing for this angle.

Cleanup, altitude, and conventions candidates use the same
`file`/`line`/`summary` shape; in `failure_scenario`, state the concrete
cost (what is duplicated, wasted, harder to maintain, or which CLAUDE.md rule
is broken) instead of a crash. Correctness bugs always outrank cleanup,
altitude, and conventions findings when the output cap forces a cut.

Pass every candidate with a nameable failure scenario through — finders that
silently drop half-believed candidates bypass the verify step and are the
dominant cause of misses.

## Phase 2 — Verify (1-vote, recall-biased)

Dedup near-duplicates (same defect, same location, same reason → keep one). For
each remaining candidate, run **one verifier** via the Agent tool:
give it the diff, the relevant file(s), and the candidate; it returns exactly
one of **CONFIRMED / PLAUSIBLE / REFUTED**.

**PLAUSIBLE by default** — do not refute a candidate for being "speculative" or
"depends on runtime state" when the state is realistic: concurrency races,
nil/undefined on a rare-but-reachable path (error handler, cold cache, missing
optional field), falsy-zero treated as missing, off-by-one on a boundary the
code does not exclude, retry storms / partial failures, regex/allowlist that
lost an anchor. These are PLAUSIBLE.

**REFUTED** only when constructible from the code: factually wrong (quote the
actual line); provably impossible (type/constant/invariant — show it); already
handled in this diff (cite the guard); or pure style with no observable effect.

Keep **CONFIRMED and PLAUSIBLE**. Drop REFUTED.

## Output

Return findings as a JSON array of at most 10 objects:

```json
[
  {
    "file": "path/to/file.ext",
    "line": 123,
    "summary": "one-sentence statement of the bug",
    "failure_scenario": "concrete inputs/state → wrong output/crash"
  }
]
```

Ranked most-severe first. If more than 10 survive, keep the 10 most
severe. If nothing survives verification, return `[]`.


## Posting to GitHub (--comment)

The `--comment` flag was passed. After producing the findings list, if the
review target is a GitHub PR, post each finding as an inline PR comment via
`mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` (one call per finding;
include a suggestion block only when it fully fixes the issue). If that tool
is not available in this session, fall back to `gh api` (repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr}/comments)
or print the findings instead. If the target is not a PR, print the findings
to the terminal and note that `--comment` was ignored.

171	    """Resolve a secret, returning None if it isn't provisioned (so the webhook
172	    surface degrades to a clear 'not set' marker instead of erroring).
173	
174	    Narrow to the store's not-found error (as the event receiver does) — an
175	    unexpected error still propagates rather than masquerading as 'not set'.
176	    """

185	) -> list[WebhookConfigRow]:
186	    """Inbound-webhook config per orchestration provider that has a connection.
187	
188	    Provider-level (one shared secret per provider), so one row per provider with
189	    ≥1 connection, listing the connections it covers. `base_url` is the public API
190	    base (scheme+host, no trailing slash). Secret-bearing for ADF — admin-only.
191	    """
192	    base = base_url.rstrip("/")
193	    names_by_provider: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
194	    for conn in session.scalars(
195	        select(Connection)
196	        .where(Connection.type.in_(ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS))
197	        .order_by(Connection.type, Connection.name)

199	        names_by_provider.setdefault(conn.type, []).append(conn.name)

203	    for provider in ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS:

213	            token_param = (

def test_admin_webhooks_dbt_row_is_not_mislabeled_as_airflow(
    client: TestClient, db_session: Any, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch
) -> None:
    # #647: the two-provider if/else dropped dbt connections into the airflow
    # branch — wrong provider label, wrong inbound endpoint, wrong signing key.
    owner = _user(db_session, "owner@x.io")
    _orch_connection(db_session, owner, ctype="dbt", name="analytics-dbt")
    db_session.commit()
    _grant_admin(monkeypatch)
    _with_store(client, _FakeStore())

    rows = {r["provider"]: r for r in client.get("/api/v1/admin/orchestration/webhooks").json()}
    assert set(rows) == {"dbt"}
    dbt = rows["dbt"]
    assert dbt["inbound_url"].endswith("/api/v1/orchestration/events/dbt")
    assert "token=" not in dbt["inbound_url"]
    assert dbt["signing_secret_name"] == "dbt-webhook-secret"
    assert "ADR 0029" in dbt["auth"]
    assert "analytics-dbt" in dbt["connection_names"]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("ctype", ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS)
def test_admin_webhooks_every_provider_yields_its_own_row(
    ctype: str, client: TestClient, db_session: Any, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch
) -> None:
    # Guards the next provider addition: a connection of each registered provider
    # must surface as a row of the SAME provider with its own events endpoint —
    # never fall through to another provider's config (#647).
    owner = _user(db_session, "owner@x.io")
    _orch_connection(db_session, owner, ctype=ctype, name=f"{ctype}-conn")
    db_session.commit()
    _grant_admin(monkeypatch)
    _with_store(client, _FakeStore())

    rows = client.get("/api/v1/admin/orchestration/webhooks").json()
    assert [r["provider"] for r in rows] == [ctype]
    assert f"/api/v1/orchestration/events/{ctype}" in rows[0]["inbound_url"]

signing-key name. The HMAC providers now dispatch through an explicit
per-provider map — a future provider missing from it fails loudly (KeyError)
instead of impersonating another provider. Adds the dbt row test + a test
parametrized over ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS, and documents
DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME in .env.app.example alongside its siblings.

Fixes #647

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin fix/issue-647-dbt-webhook-config && gh pr create --title "fix(admin): webhook-config rows dispatch per provider — dbt no longer mislabeled as airflow (#647)" --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "## Summary
- \`admin_service.webhook_configs()\` kept the #490 two-provider \`if adf / else airflow\` shape after ADR 0029 widened \`ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS\` to include dbt — so a dbt connection surfaced in the admin Webhooks tab as \`provider=\"airflow\"\` with the **wrong inbound endpoint** and **wrong signing-secret name**.
- HMAC-callback providers (airflow, dbt) now dispatch through an explicit per-provider map carrying the signing-key setting + ADR; an unmapped future provider raises \`KeyError\` instead of silently impersonating another provider.
- \`DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME\` documented in \`.env.app.example\` next to its ADF/Airflow siblings (config.py default \`dbt-webhook-secret\` unchanged).

## Acceptance criteria (from #647)
- [x] \`webhook_configs()\` dispatches per provider — dbt rows carry \`provider=\"dbt\"\`, the \`/orchestration/events/dbt\` URL, and \`settings.dbt_webhook_secret_name\`
- [x] Test creates a dbt connection and asserts its row; plus a test parametrized over \`ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS\` guarding the next provider addition
- [x] \`DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME\` documented in \`.env.app.example\`

## Test plan
- [x] Black / Ruff / mypy / Bandit clean on changed files
- [x] Full backend suite: 1467 passed, 98.4% coverage

ADR 0029 added dbt as the third OrchestrationProvider but three frontend
surfaces still hardcoded the original two: the Suite Triggers provider
dropdown (a suite could not be bound to a dbt build from the UI), the
Pipeline-runs provider filter, and the PipelineRun/listPipelineRuns types.
The provider tuple + labels are now single-sourced from
api/triggerBindings.ts (mirroring the backend tuple), the Results filter
builds its options from it, and Settings' webhook rows reuse the shared
labels — with dbt-correct 'post-build callback' copy and the
missing-secret tag no longer ADF-only (pairs with the #680 backend fix).
Tests iterate the shared tuple so the next provider addition is caught.

Fixes #652

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin fix/issue-652-dbt-frontend-parity && gh pr create --title "fix(frontend): dbt provider in Triggers dropdown, Pipeline-runs filter + admin Webhooks (#652)" --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "## Summary
- \`ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS\` / \`PROVIDER_LABELS\` in \`api/triggerBindings.ts\` now include **dbt** (mirroring the backend tuple, ADR 0029) — the Suite Triggers provider dropdown offers it, so a suite can finally be bound to a successful dbt build from the UI.
- Pipeline-runs provider filter builds its options from the shared tuple (and gets an accessible name); \`PipelineRun.provider\` / \`listPipelineRuns\` params are typed off the shared \`OrchestrationProvider\` instead of a stale two-provider union.
- Settings → Webhooks now reuses the shared \`PROVIDER_LABELS\` (dbt rows were falling back to the raw provider string), shows **post-build callback** copy for dbt instead of Airflow's \"DAG callback\", and the \"webhook secret not set\" tag is no longer ADF-only (renders for HMAC rows too — pairs with the #680 backend fix that makes \`token_configured\` honest for those rows). These two were verified findings from the #680 code review, folded here since they're this PR's surface.

## Test plan
- [x] TriggersPanel + Results filter tests iterate the shared provider tuple (guards the next provider addition), plus a dbt filter round-trip
- [x] Settings tests: dbt row label/copy + missing-secret tag on an HMAC row
- [x] Prettier / ESLint / tsc / full Vitest suite green (376 tests)

Fixes #652

@pytest.mark.parametrize("ctype", ["airflow", "dbt"])
def test_admin_webhooks_hmac_rows_mark_missing_signing_secret(
    ctype: str, client: TestClient, db_session: Any, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch
) -> None:
    # token_configured must reflect the signing key's actual presence in the
    # store — a hardcoded True hid the misconfiguration until callbacks 401'd.
    owner = _user(db_session, "owner@x.io")
    _orch_connection(db_session, owner, ctype=ctype, name=f"{ctype}-conn")
    db_session.commit()
    _grant_admin(monkeypatch)
    _with_store(client, _FakeStore(token=None))  # signing key not provisioned

    [row] = client.get("/api/v1/admin/orchestration/webhooks").json()
    assert row["token_configured"] is False


def test_admin_webhooks_marks_missing_secret(
    client: TestClient, db_session: Any, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch
) -> None:
    owner = _user(db_session, "owner@x.io")
    _orch_connection(db_session, owner, ctype="adf", name="prod-factory")

input.command:
gh pr comment 680 --body "## /code-review findings (high effort, 2 grouped finder agents + source verification)

**Fixed in the fixup commit (5a132b16):**
1. **CONFIRMED — silent misconfig:** \`token_configured\` was hardcoded \`True\` for HMAC rows (pre-existing for airflow, duplicated onto dbt) — an unprovisioned signing key showed as configured until callbacks 401'd at the receiver. Now reflects the secret store (\`_safe_secret\`), parametrized test added. The frontend half (showing the missing-secret tag on HMAC rows) landed in #681.
2. **CONFIRMED — doc parity:** \`DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME\` was added to the dev template but missing from \`deploy/.env.app.prod.example\` and the Terraform api env block. Both now list it.

**Fixed in #681 (this PR's frontend consumers):**
3. Settings webhook rows: dbt had no \`PROVIDER_LABELS\` entry (raw-string fallback) and the helper copy said 'DAG callback snippet' for dbt rows — now shared labels + post-build wording.

**Deferred (filed as a follow-up issue):**
4. **Altitude:** the \`hmac_providers\` dict is a third hand-maintained home for per-provider webhook-auth knowledge (alongside the per-provider authenticators in \`orchestration.py\` and the Settings fields). A declarative webhook-auth descriptor on the \`OrchestrationProvider\` interface/registry would make this loop fully generic — and would also turn the remaining KeyError-on-provider-4 failure mode into a can't-happen. Narrowing-not-introducing here, so deferred per the working agreements.

**Consciously not changed:** the overlap between the dedicated dbt/airflow tests and the parametrized guard — the dedicated tests document provider-specific nuances (URL-token vs HMAC) that a table-driven merge would obscure." > /dev/null && gh issue create --title "refactor(orchestration): move webhook-auth metadata onto the OrchestrationProvider seam (admin webhook-config dispatch)" --label "tech-debt" --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "Follow-up from the #680 code review (which fixed #647's dbt-as-airflow mislabeling).

\`admin_service.webhook_configs()\` now dispatches HMAC providers through a local \`hmac_providers\` dict — correct, but it's a third hand-maintained home for per-provider webhook-auth knowledge, alongside:
- the per-provider authenticators in \`backend/app/api/v1/orchestration.py\` (\`_authenticate_airflow\` / \`_authenticate_dbt\`, each reading its own \`settings.*_webhook_secret_name\`)
- the \`Settings\` fields themselves

CLAUDE.md §11: provider-specific branching in service code should go through the \`OrchestrationProvider\` abstraction. The Protocol already carries declarative metadata (\`provider\`, \`resource_config_key\`); a webhook-auth descriptor (auth mode \`url_token | hmac\`, ADR ref, settings secret-name attribute) on the provider class/registry would:
- make the \`webhook_configs()\` loop fully generic (the ADF if-branch becomes data too)
- eliminate the remaining failure mode where provider #4 lands in \`ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS\` but not in the dict → \`KeyError\` → the whole admin webhooks endpoint 500s once any connection of the new type exists (the parametrized test in #680 catches this at test time, but only via a fixture crash)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Webhook-auth descriptor lives on the provider interface/registry (one home)
- [ ] \`webhook_configs()\` has no per-provider branching or local provider map
- [ ] \`orchestration.py\` receivers resolve the signing secret via the same descriptor
- [ ] Existing #680 tests still pass unchanged (behavior identical)" 2>&1 | tail -1

gh issue create --title "refactor(orchestration): move webhook-auth metadata onto the OrchestrationProvider seam (admin webhook-config dispatch)" --label "refactor" --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "Follow-up from the #680 code review (which fixed #647's dbt-as-airflow mislabeling).

\`admin_service.webhook_configs()\` now dispatches HMAC providers through a local \`hmac_providers\` dict — correct, but it's a third hand-maintained home for per-provider webhook-auth knowledge, alongside:
- the per-provider authenticators in \`backend/app/api/v1/orchestration.py\` (\`_authenticate_airflow\` / \`_authenticate_dbt\`, each reading its own \`settings.*_webhook_secret_name\`)
- the \`Settings\` fields themselves

CLAUDE.md §11: provider-specific branching in service code should go through the \`OrchestrationProvider\` abstraction. The Protocol already carries declarative metadata (\`provider\`, \`resource_config_key\`); a webhook-auth descriptor (auth mode \`url_token | hmac\`, ADR ref, settings secret-name attribute) on the provider class/registry would:
- make the \`webhook_configs()\` loop fully generic (the ADF if-branch becomes data too)
- eliminate the remaining failure mode where provider #4 lands in \`ORCHESTRATION_PROVIDERS\` but not in the dict → \`KeyError\` → the whole admin webhooks endpoint 500s once any connection of the new type exists (the #680 parametrized test catches this at test time, but only via a fixture crash)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Webhook-auth descriptor lives on the provider interface/registry (one home)
- [ ] \`webhook_configs()\` has no per-provider branching or local provider map
- [ ] \`orchestration.py\` receivers resolve the signing secret via the same descriptor
- [ ] Existing #680 tests still pass unchanged (behavior identical)" 2>&1 | tail -1

Connections have had a version-history read API since W4 with zero frontend
callers — asymmetric with checks, whose #280 history drawer ships a working
History button in the editor. Adds the ConnectionVersion client +
listConnectionVersions, a ConnectionHistoryDrawer mirroring the check
drawer's UX (newest-first immutable snapshots, Current tag, credential-free
config JSON, empty state for pre-versioning rows), and a History button on
the connection edit page.

Fixes #654

- Adds the missing frontend surface for \`GET /connections/{id}/versions\` (#654): a **History** button + drawer on the connection edit page, mirroring the check-version-history drawer (#280) for UI/UX consistency — same newest-first snapshot list, \`Current\` tag, author + timestamp line, and pretty-printed credential-free config JSON.

The #370 backend snooze (POST/DELETE .../checks/{id}/snooze) had zero

alert_snoozed_until to the Check client type (every checks response already
returned it), snoozeCheck/clearCheckSnooze client calls, a Snooze dropdown
(1h / 24h / 7d) + Unsnooze action on each check row, and an orange

timestamp reads as active — matching the backend semantics).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin feature/issue-653-snooze-ui && gh pr create --title "feat(frontend): per-check alert snooze/unsnooze UI (#653)" --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "## Summary
- Exposes the Week-6 backend per-check alert snooze (#370) in the UI: each check row in the suite detail panel gets a **Snooze** dropdown (1 hour / 24 hours / 7 days — well under the backend's 720h cap) or an **Unsnooze** action when already snoozed, plus an orange **Snoozed until <ts>** badge with a tooltip clarifying results still record.
- \`Check\` client type gains \`alert_snoozed_until\` (the API already returned it); \`snoozeCheck\` / \`clearCheckSnooze\` call the existing edit-gated endpoints.
- An expired snooze timestamp renders as active (no badge, Snooze offered) — matching backend semantics.

## Test plan
- [x] Snooze round-trip: dropdown → \`POST .../snooze {hours: 24}\` → refreshed list shows the badge
- [x] Unsnooze round-trip: badge + Unsnooze → \`DELETE .../snooze\` → badge gone
- [x] Expired-snooze edge: past timestamp = active

content.text: Filing the retention follow-up issue and checking PR states:

input.command:
gh issue create --title "feat(connections): purge/redact path for connection version history (immutable snapshots retain edited-out config values)" --label "enhancement" --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "Follow-up from the #683 code review (connection version-history UI, #654).

Connection version snapshots (\`connection_versions\`) are immutable, credential-free, and retained until the connection is deleted. \`GET /connections/{id}/versions\` is any-authenticated-user (consistent with the rest of the connections surface). The residual exposure, now user-visible via the #654 History drawer:

A user who mistakenly pastes a sensitive value (token, hostname-with-embedded-credential) into a **config** field — instead of the secret field — and then edits it out leaves that value readable by every authenticated workspace user indefinitely. There is no purge/redact path short of deleting the whole connection; the W5 retention sweep covers \`results.sample_failures\`, not connection versions.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A way to remove/redact a specific connection version (or all versions), gated appropriately (owner/workspace-admin)
- [ ] Decide + document the same question for check versions (\`check_versions\` has the identical property)
- [ ] Test covering the purge path

Severity: low (requires user error to matter) — filed per the no-silent-deferral rule." 2>&1 | tail -1; for pr in 679 681 683 684; do gh pr view $pr --json number,state -q '"\(.number): \(.state)"'; done

164	    """Append an immutable snapshot of `check`'s current state as its next
165	    version (a per-check sequence starting at 1). The caller commits — this only
166	    adds the row, so the snapshot and the create/update it records commit

168	    against a concurrent double-write computing the same number (rare under v1's
169	    single-tenant, low-concurrency editing).
170	
171	    `check.id` must be populated (flush or commit the check first).
172	    """
173	    # MAX over no rows is NULL → None; `or 0` makes the first version 1.
174	    current_max = session.scalar(

"""Catalog↔GX contract test (#205).

The check editor's ``expectationCatalog.ts`` is the frontend's source of truth
for expectation ``type`` (snake_case → GX class) and each config field name
(→ GX kwarg). The backend deliberately has NO server catalog — config is
free-form kwargs title-cased to a GX class in ``gx_runner`` — so this coupling
has zero compile-time check: a catalog typo or a GX point-release kwarg rename
ships fine and only fails at suite-run time on the worker.

This test pins the seam against the PINNED GX version, resolving each catalog
entry through the very same ``_expectation_class_name``/``getattr`` path the
runner uses. The input is ``tests/fixtures/expectation_catalog.json``, kept in
lock-step with the live TS catalog by the frontend drift-guard
(``frontend/tests/components/catalogContract.test.ts`` — regenerate with
``UPDATE_CATALOG_FIXTURE=1``). A GX bump or a catalog edit that breaks the
pairing now fails HERE, in CI, not on the worker.
"""

import functools
import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import great_expectations.expectations as gxe
import pytest

from backend.app.datasources import monitors
from backend.app.datasources.gx_runner import _expectation_class_name
from backend.app.services.custom_sql import CUSTOM_SQL_EXPECTATION_TYPE, QUERY_KEY

_FIXTURE = Path(__file__).parent.parent / "fixtures" / "expectation_catalog.json"


@functools.cache
def _catalog() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Parse lazily (and once): a missing/corrupt fixture must fail THESE tests,
    not abort the whole session as a collection error."""
    with _FIXTURE.open() as f:
        data: list[dict[str, Any]] = json.load(f)
    return data


def _expectations() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    return [e for e in _catalog() if e["kind"] == "expectation"]


def _monitors() -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    return [e for e in _catalog() if e["kind"] != "expectation"]


# Parametrization ids come from a SEPARATE minimal read that tolerates a broken
# fixture (falling back to indices), so collection itself never hard-fails.
def _expectation_params() -> list[Any]:
    try:
        entries = _expectations()
    except Exception:
        # Broken fixture: emit one placeholder param so the body's _catalog()
        # call reports the parse error as an ordinary test failure.
        return [pytest.param(None, id="fixture-unreadable")]
    return [pytest.param(e, id=e["type"]) for e in entries]

283	    argument means "not provided", so an omitted field is left unchanged. v1 has
284	    no clear-to-NULL path for thresholds; recreate the check to drop one.
285	    """
286	    check = get_check(session, suite_id, check_id)
287	    if name is not None:
288	        check.name = name

293	    if warn_threshold is not None:
294	        check.warn_threshold = warn_threshold
295	    if fail_threshold is not None:
296	        check.fail_threshold = fail_threshold
297	    if critical_threshold is not None:
298	        check.critical_threshold = critical_threshold
299	    # Re-validate against the post-patch state: a PATCH may change only the config
300	    # or only a threshold, so guard the effective check before commit (a rejected
301	    # update persists nothing). `kind` is immutable on update, so it's read off the
302	    # existing check.
303	    if check.kind in MONITOR_KINDS:
304	        suite = get_suite(session, suite_id)
305	        validate_monitor_check(
306	            check.kind,

310	            fail_threshold=check.fail_threshold,

312	        )

319	        )
320	    # Only snapshot a real change: a no-op PATCH (empty body, or fields set to
321	    # their current values) must not mint a duplicate version — that would fill
322	    # the history drawer with noise and defeat "see previous config". SQLAlchemy
323	    # reports net changes, so setting a field to its existing value isn't dirty.
324	    if session.is_modified(check):
325	        record_check_version(session, check, actor_id=actor_id)
326	    try:
327	        session.commit()
328	    except IntegrityError as exc:
329	        # Roll back the poisoned tx, then map ONLY the version-snapshot collision to
330	        # a 409 (reload + retry): two concurrent edits computed the same next
331	        # `version_no` and raced on the `uq_check_versions_check_version` backstop.
332	        # Any other IntegrityError (a different constraint) is not a concurrency
333	        # conflict — re-raise it rather than mislabel it "edited concurrently".
334	        session.rollback()
335	        if _VERSION_UNIQUE_CONSTRAINT not in str(exc.orig):
336	            raise
337	        raise CheckEditConflictError(
338	            "this check was edited concurrently — reload and retry",
339	            detail={"check_id": str(check_id)},
340	        ) from exc
341	    session.refresh(check)

import great_expectations.expectations as gxe
from great_expectations.expectations.expectation import Expectation

def pas(t): return "".join(p.title() for p in t.split("_"))

# case 1: unknown type
print("1:", getattr(gxe, pas("expect_totally_made_up_thing"), None))
# case 2: missing required column
try:
    gxe.ExpectColumnValuesToBeBetween()
    print("2: ACCEPTED (no column)")
except Exception as e:
    print("2: rejected:", type(e).__name__, str(e)[:120])
# case 2b: column present, no bounds
try:
    gxe.ExpectColumnValuesToBeBetween(column="a")
    print("2b: ACCEPTED (both bounds None)")
except Exception as e:
    print("2b: rejected:", type(e).__name__, str(e)[:120])
# case 3: wrong types
try:
    gxe.ExpectColumnValuesToBeBetween(column="a", min_value="not-a-number", max_value=[])
    print("3: ACCEPTED (wrong types)")
except Exception as e:
    print("3: rejected:", type(e).__name__, str(e)[:150])
# extra key behavior
try:
    gxe.ExpectColumnValuesToNotBeNull(column="a", bogus_key=1)
    print("4: ACCEPTED extra key")
except Exception as e:
    print("4: rejected:", type(e).__name__, str(e)[:120])
# base-class guard: is every gxe attr an Expectation subclass?
import typing
print("5: Any attr:", getattr(gxe, "Any", "absent") is typing.Any)
print("6: base ok:", issubclass(gxe.ExpectColumnValuesToBeBetween, Expectation))
# coercion: string number
try:
    e = gxe.ExpectColumnValuesToBeBetween(column="a", min_value="5")
    print("7: coerced min_value='5' ->", repr(e.min_value))
except Exception as ex:
    print("7: rejected:", str(ex)[:100])
EOF

input.new_string:
"""Check CRUD — checks are GX expectations nested under a suite.

A check belongs to exactly one suite (FK + cascade). This layer validates the
suite exists, enforces the v1 monitor-kind limit, and validates the check's
`config` at author time: expectation-kind checks resolve + construct their GX
expectation class (#651 — the same translation the runner performs, pulled
forward so garbage 422s instead of persisting and only failing at run time);
validation against live data remains the dry-run path, not CRUD.

input.new_string:
def _has_positive_threshold(fail: Decimal | None, critical: Decimal | None) -> bool:
    """Whether a fail or critical threshold is set to a positive value."""
    return (fail is not None and fail > 0) or (critical is not None and critical > 0)


# Longest string allowed anywhere in an expectation config. Generous for real
# kwargs (column names, value-set members, regexes) while blocking the
# 100KB-column-name class of junk GX itself accepts (#651). Custom-SQL queries
# are validated (and bounded) separately and never reach this walk.
_CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS = 1_000


def _find_oversized_string(value: Any, path: str = "config") -> str | None:
    """Depth-first search for a string over the cap; returns its path, or None."""
    if isinstance(value, str):
        return path if len(value) > _CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS else None
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        for key, item in value.items():
            found = _find_oversized_string(item, f"{path}.{key}")
            if found:
                return found
    if isinstance(value, list):
        for i, item in enumerate(value):
            found = _find_oversized_string(item, f"{path}[{i}]")
            if found:
                return found
    return None


def validate_expectation_check(expectation_type: str, config: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    """Author-time validation for `kind='expectation'` checks (#651).

    Resolves and constructs the GX expectation exactly like the runner
    (`gx_runner._to_gx_expectation`), so an unknown `expectation_type`, a
    missing/wrong-typed/extra config key — anything that would fail on the
    worker — 422s at create/update/import instead of persisting. GX expectation
    classes are pydantic models, so construction IS the schema validation.
    Custom-SQL checks (ADR 0019) have their own validator and must not be passed
    here (their type is not a GX class).
    """
    oversized = _find_oversized_string(config)
    if oversized is not None:
        raise CheckConfigInvalidError(
            f"config value at {oversized} exceeds {_CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS} characters",
            detail={"path": oversized, "max_chars": _CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS},
        )

    # Lazy: importing great_expectations is heavy (seconds), and the API process
    # only needs it on the authoring paths — same pattern as the vault client.
    import great_expectations.expectations as gxe
    from great_expectations.expectations.expectation import Expectation

    from backend.app.datasources.gx_runner import _expectation_class_name

    class_name = _expectation_class_name(expectation_type)
    expectation_cls = getattr(gxe, class_name, None)
    # The issubclass guard keeps a crafted type from resolving to a non-expectation
    # module attribute via the title-casing getattr.
    if expectation_cls is None or not (
        isinstance(expectation_cls, type) and issubclass(expectation_cls, Expectation)
    ):
        raise CheckConfigInvalidError(
            f"unknown expectation_type {expectation_type!r} — not a Great Expectations "
            "expectation",
            detail={"expectation_type": expectation_type},
        )
    try:
        expectation_cls(**config)
    except Exception as exc:
        # pydantic ValidationError (missing/wrong-typed/extra kwargs) or a GX
        # root-validator error; the message is user-actionable, so surface it.
        raise CheckConfigInvalidError(
            f"invalid config for {expectation_type}: {str(exc)[:500]}",
            detail={"expectation_type": expectation_type},
        ) from exc

# ───────────────────────── expectation-kind validation (#651) ──────


def test_create_rejects_unknown_expectation_type(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # Not a GX expectation → 422, never 201 (previously persisted silently).
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks",
        json=_payload(expectation_type="expect_totally_made_up_thing"),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json()["error"]["code"] == "check_config_invalid"
    assert "expect_totally_made_up_thing" in resp.json()["error"]["message"]


def test_create_rejects_missing_required_config_keys(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # expect_column_values_to_be_between with an empty config lacks the
    # required `column` (and both bounds) — GX construction fails → 422.
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks",
        json=_payload(expectation_type="expect_column_values_to_be_between", config={}),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json()["error"]["code"] == "check_config_invalid"


def test_create_rejects_both_bounds_missing(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # GX's own root validator: min_value and max_value cannot both be None.
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks",
        json=_payload(
            expectation_type="expect_column_values_to_be_between", config={"column": "amount"}
        ),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422


def test_create_rejects_wrong_typed_config_values(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks",
        json=_payload(
            expectation_type="expect_column_values_to_be_between",
            config={"column": "amount", "min_value": "not-a-number", "max_value": []},
        ),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert resp.json()["error"]["code"] == "check_config_invalid"


def test_create_rejects_unknown_config_keys(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # GX expectations forbid extra kwargs — a typo'd key must not persist.
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks",
        json=_payload(config={"column": "order_id", "colunm_typo": "x"}),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422


def test_create_rejects_oversized_config_string(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # A 100KB "column name" previously persisted; the size cap 422s it —
    # including when nested inside a list (value_set-style).
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    huge = "x" * 100_000
    flat = client.post(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks", json=_payload(config={"column": huge}))
    assert flat.status_code == 422
    nested = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks",
        json=_payload(
            expectation_type="expect_column_values_to_be_in_set",
            config={"column": "order_id", "value_set": ["ok", huge]},
        ),
    )
    assert nested.status_code == 422


def test_update_revalidates_expectation_config(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # PATCH must apply the same gate on the post-patch state: a valid check
    # cannot be edited into garbage, and a rejected PATCH persists nothing.
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    created = client.post(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks", json=_payload())
    cid = created.json()["id"]

    bad_type = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks/{cid}", json={"expectation_type": "expect_nonsense"}
    )
    assert bad_type.status_code == 422
    bad_config = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks/{cid}", json={"config": {"column": "order_id", "bogus": 1}}
    )
    assert bad_config.status_code == 422
    unchanged = client.get(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks/{cid}").json()
    assert unchanged["expectation_type"] == "expect_column_values_to_not_be_null"
    assert unchanged["config"] == {"column": "order_id"}


def test_import_rejects_invalid_expectation_check(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # The import path must not smuggle in a check a direct POST would 422.
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    exported = client.get(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/export").json()
    exported["suite"]["checks"] = [
        {
            "name": "junk",
            "kind": "expectation",
            "expectation_type": "expect_totally_made_up_thing",
            "config": {},
            "warn_threshold": None,
            "fail_threshold": None,
            "critical_threshold": None,
        }
    ]
    resp = client.post(
        "/api/v1/suites/import",
        json={"document": exported, "connection_id": exported["suite"]["connection_id"]},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422

def export_suite(
    suite_id: uuid.UUID,
    current_user: Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> SuiteDocument:
    suite = require_permission(db, suite_id, current_user.id, minimum="view")
    return SuiteDocument.model_validate(suite_io.export_suite(suite))


@router.post(
    "/suites/import",
    response_model=SuiteRead,
    status_code=status.HTTP_201_CREATED,
    summary="Import a suite document onto a connection",
)
def import_suite(
    payload: SuiteImportRequest,
    current_user: Annotated[User, Depends(get_current_user)],
    db: Annotated[Session, Depends(get_db)],
) -> SuiteRead:
    # Like create_suite: any authenticated user may import; the new suite is
    # owned by them. Thresholds/config round-trip exactly (Decimal in/out).
    doc = payload.document
    suite = suite_io.import_suite(
        db,
        version=doc.version,
        name=doc.name,
        description=doc.description,
        checks=[c.model_dump() for c in doc.checks],
        connection_id=payload.connection_id,
        created_by=current_user.id,
    )
    return SuiteRead.model_validate(suite)

    name: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=256)

    name: str = Field(min_length=1, max_length=128)
    description: str | None = Field(default=None, max_length=1024)
    checks: list[CheckDocument] = Field(default_factory=list)

def test_import_rejects_invalid_expectation_check(client: TestClient, db_session: Any) -> None:
    # The import path must not smuggle in a check a direct POST would 422 —
    # and it is atomic, so the bad document writes no suite at all.
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    suite = client.get(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}").json()
    document = {
        "version": 1,
        "name": "smuggled",
        "description": None,
        "checks": [
            {
                "name": "junk",
                "kind": "expectation",
                "expectation_type": "expect_totally_made_up_thing",
                "config": {},
            }
        ],
    }
    resp = client.post(
        "/api/v1/suites/import",
        json={"document": document, "connection_id": suite["connection_id"]},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    names = [s["name"] for s in client.get("/api/v1/suites").json()]
    assert "smuggled" not in names

check = get_check(session, suite_id, check_id)
    # Compute the effective post-patch values and validate them BEFORE touching
    # the ORM object: a rejected update must leave nothing dirty in the session
    # (mutate-then-raise would let a later commit on the same session persist
    # the invalid state). `kind` is immutable on update, so it's read off the
    # existing check.
    new_expectation_type = (
        expectation_type if expectation_type is not None else check.expectation_type
    )
    new_config = config if config is not None else check.config
    new_fail = fail_threshold if fail_threshold is not None else check.fail_threshold
    new_critical = critical_threshold if critical_threshold is not None else check.critical_threshold
    if check.kind in MONITOR_KINDS:
        suite = get_suite(session, suite_id)
        validate_monitor_check(
            check.kind,
            new_config,
            expectation_type=new_expectation_type,
            connection_type=_connection_type(session, suite),
            fail_threshold=new_fail,
            critical_threshold=new_critical,
        )
    elif is_custom_sql(new_expectation_type):
        suite = get_suite(session, suite_id)
        validate_custom_sql_check(
            expectation_type=new_expectation_type,
            config=new_config,
            connection_type=_connection_type(session, suite),
        )
    else:
        validate_expectation_check(new_expectation_type, new_config)

    if name is not None:
        check.name = name
    if expectation_type is not None:
        check.expectation_type = expectation_type
    if config is not None:
        check.config = config
    if warn_threshold is not None:
        check.warn_threshold = warn_threshold

config keys, and 100KB string values — all only failing later on the
worker. validate_expectation_check now resolves + constructs the GX
expectation exactly like the runner (gx_runner's class translation; GX
expectations are pydantic models, so construction IS the schema check),
behind a per-string size cap GX doesn't enforce, with GX imported lazily
(heavy import, authoring-only paths). Wired into create, update, and
suite-import — import can't smuggle what POST 422s.

update_check now validates the effective post-patch values BEFORE mutating
the ORM object: the old mutate-then-raise left a rejected PATCH dirty in
the session (caught by the new regression test).

Fixes #651

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin fix/issue-651-expectation-validation && gh pr create --title "fix(checks): author-time GX validation for expectation-kind checks (#651)" --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "## Summary
- \`kind='expectation'\` authoring now validates at create/update/import instead of persisting garbage that only fails on the worker (#651, P1): \`validate_expectation_check\` resolves the GX class through the **same** \`gx_runner\` translation the run path uses and constructs it — GX expectations are pydantic models, so construction enforces required keys, value types, and rejects unknown/extra kwargs. An \`issubclass(Expectation)\` guard keeps a crafted type from resolving to a non-expectation module attribute.
- Adds a per-string config size cap (1000 chars, recursive walk) — GX happily accepts a 100KB column name.
- GX is imported **lazily** inside the validator (heavy import; authoring-only paths — same pattern as the vault client, #523).
- The suite-import path applies the same gate (atomic — a bad document writes nothing).
- **Bonus fix caught by the new regression test:** \`update_check\` mutated the ORM object before validating, leaving a rejected PATCH dirty in the session; it now validates the effective post-patch values first (all three validators: monitor / custom-SQL / expectation).

## Repro coverage (all four #651 cases, at the API layer)
- [x] \`expect_totally_made_up_thing\` → 422 (was 201)
- [x] \`expect_column_values_to_be_between\` with \`{}\` → 422 (missing \`column\`, both bounds None)
- [x] wrong-typed \`min_value\`/\`max_value\` → 422
- [x] 100KB string (flat + nested in \`value_set\`) → 422
- [x] plus: unknown config key, PATCH revalidation + nothing-persisted, import smuggling

## Test plan
- [x] Black / Ruff / mypy / Bandit clean
- [x] Full backend suite: 1477 passed, 98.4% coverage (was 1467 — 8 new #651 tests + 2 from #680)

Fixes #651

pnpm exec eslint src/components/checks/snooze.tsx && pnpm vitest run 2>&1 | grep -E "Tests |failed" && cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ && git add frontend && git commit -m "fix(frontend): snooze UI follow-ups — honest copy, edit-gating, expiry ticker, run-detail badge (#653)

Post-merge /code-review on #684 (four confirmed findings):
1. The badge tooltip claimed the check's alerts are 'muted until then' —
   suppression is decided per run (an alert is muted only when EVERY failing
   check is snoozed), so the copy now says a run alert still fires if other
   checks fail.
2. Snooze/Unsnooze are edit-gated on the backend but rendered for view-only
   users; ChecksList now takes the edit capability and hides them (matching
   the sibling Triggers/Schedules panels). Badge stays visible to viewers.
3. A snooze expiring while the page stayed open kept its badge/Unsnooze
   until a manual refresh; a 60s ticker re-evaluates expiry.
4. Run detail — the failure-triage surface — now shows the snoozed badge on
   each check row (isSnoozed/SnoozedTag extracted to a shared module).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin fix/snooze-ui-review-followups && gh pr create --title "fix(frontend): snooze UI follow-ups from the #684 post-merge review (#653)" --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "## Summary
Four confirmed findings from the post-merge /code-review of #684 (the #653 snooze UI):
1. **Honest suppression copy** — suppression is run-level (\`alerting/dispatch\` mutes only when *every* failing check is snoozed); the tooltip no longer implies per-check muting.
2. **Edit-gating** — Snooze/Unsnooze hide for view-only users (backend 403s them anyway; matches the sibling panels' convention). The badge stays visible to viewers. Pre-existing ungated Edit/Delete row actions left as-is (viewer's check editor is a legitimate read surface; backend gates writes).
3. **Expiry ticker** — a 60s interval re-evaluates \`isSnoozed\`, so a snooze lapsing while the page is open drops its badge/action instead of misreporting live suppression state.
4. **Run-detail badge** — the triage surface now marks snoozed checks (\`isSnoozed\`/\`SnoozedTag\` extracted to \`components/checks/snooze.tsx\`).

## Test plan
- [x] Viewer test: badge visible, controls hidden
- [x] Run-detail snoozed-badge test
- [x] Existing snooze round-trips updated to edit-capable fixtures
- [x] Prettier / ESLint / tsc / full Vitest suite green

python3 - <<'EOF'
import re
targets = {
    "frontend/src/components/suites/SchedulesPanel.tsx": ["<Table<Schedule>"],
    "frontend/src/components/checks/ColumnProfilePanel.tsx": ["<Table"],
    "frontend/src/components/dashboard/RecentRuns.tsx": ["<Table<Run>"],
    "frontend/src/components/profile/ApiKeysPanel.tsx": ["<Table<ApiKey>"],
    "frontend/src/pages/Results.tsx": ["<Table<Run>", "<Table<PipelineRun>"],
    "frontend/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx": ["<Table<Record<string, unknown>>", "<Table\n"],
    "frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx": ["<Table\n"],
}
# Add scroll={{ x: 'max-content' }} right after each opening Table tag line.
for path, tags in targets.items():
    src = open(path).read()
    lines = src.split("\n")
    out = []
    for line in lines:
        out.append(line)
        stripped = line.strip()
        if stripped in ("<Table", "<Table<Schedule>", "<Table<Run>", "<Table<ApiKey>", "<Table<PipelineRun>", "<Table<Record<string, unknown>>"):
            indent = line[: len(line) - len(line.lstrip())] + "  "
            out.append(f"{indent}scroll={{{{ x: 'max-content' }}}}")
    open(path, "w").write("\n".join(out))
    print(path, "done")
EOF

    assert 'justify="space-between" align="center" gap={12}' in lines[i], (path, lines[i])

   replaced by a header hamburger: trigger={null}, controlled collapse,
   onBreakpoint drives visibility, and a nav click re-collapses on
   narrow screens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -u origin fix/issue-617-mobile-responsive && gh pr create --title "fix(frontend): responsive layouts for narrow viewports (#617)" --milestone "v1.1 Week 2 — Portability: OTel logs, secrets lifecycle, dry-run depth" --body "## Summary
All four bugs from the #617 mobile sweep, verified live against the dev stack via Playwright at **390×844** (and desktop 1280×800 for regression):

1. **Suite-detail char-per-line (critical):** the master-detail now stacks vertically below \`md\` (\`Grid.useBreakpoint\`, \`=== false\` so desktop never flashes the stacked layout) — and the detail header row (\`title + Run/Share/Export/Edit/Delete\`) wraps with a min-width title block, which was the second half of the squeeze. Measured: title went from 58×112px to 239×28px.
2. **Tables clip with no scrollbar:** \`scroll={{ x: 'max-content' }}\` on every data table (Results runs + pipeline runs, run-detail results + sample rows, Admin, dashboard recent runs, schedules panel, column profiler, API-keys panel). Measured: runs table scrolls 1169px inside a 326px wrapper; body never scrolls horizontally.
3. **☰ over headings:** AntD's default zero-width Sider trigger is gone (\`trigger={null}\`); a hamburger lives in the Header (reserved space), collapse is controlled, \`onBreakpoint\` drives it, and a nav click re-collapses on narrow screens. Verified: open → 220px, nav click → collapsed.
4. **Page headers:** title+action rows \`wrap\` on all pages (Connections was the clearest repro).

## Test plan
- [x] Live Playwright verification at 390×844: suite-detail title one line, tables scroll in-container, no floating trigger, headers wrap, \`document.body.scrollWidth\` stays 390 on every checked route
- [x] Desktop regression at 1280×800: sider expanded, no hamburger, side-by-side master-detail
- [x] Prettier / ESLint / tsc / full Vitest suite green (one assertion updated: the scroll structure duplicates table header cells)

Note: the issue also suggests a mobile-viewport Playwright e2e project as a regression guard — deferred (doubles the e2e lane's runtime); can be filed if wanted.

result.output:
160	
161	
162	# Longest string allowed anywhere in an expectation config. Generous for real
163	# kwargs (column names, value-set members, regexes) while blocking the
164	# 100KB-column-name class of junk GX itself accepts (#651). Custom-SQL queries
165	# are validated (and bounded) separately and never reach this walk.
166	_CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS = 1_000
167	
168	
169	def _find_oversized_string(value: Any, path: str = "config") -> str | None:
170	    """Depth-first search for a string over the cap; returns its path, or None."""
171	    if isinstance(value, str):
172	        return path if len(value) > _CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS else None
173	    if isinstance(value, dict):
174	        for key, item in value.items():
175	            found = _find_oversized_string(item, f"{path}.{key}")
176	            if found:
177	                return found
178	    if isinstance(value, list):
179	        for i, item in enumerate(value):
180	            found = _find_oversized_string(item, f"{path}[{i}]")
181	            if found:
182	                return found
183	    return None
184	
185	
186	def validate_expectation_check(expectation_type: str, config: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
187	    """Author-time validation for `kind='expectation'` checks (#651).
188	
189	    Resolves and constructs the GX expectation exactly like the runner
190	    (`gx_runner._to_gx_expectation`), so an unknown `expectation_type`, a
191	    missing/wrong-typed/extra config key — anything that would fail on the
192	    worker — 422s at create/update/import instead of persisting. GX expectation
193	    classes are pydantic models, so construction IS the schema validation.
194	    Custom-SQL checks (ADR 0019) have their own validator and must not be passed
195	    here (their type is not a GX class).
196	    """
197	    oversized = _find_oversized_string(config)
198	    if oversized is not None:
199	        raise CheckConfigInvalidError(
200	            f"config value at {oversized} exceeds {_CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS} characters",
201	            detail={"path": oversized, "max_chars": _CONFIG_STRING_MAX_CHARS},
202	        )
203	
204	    # Lazy: importing great_expectations is heavy (seconds), and the API process
205	    # only needs it on the authoring paths — same pattern as the vault client.
206	    import great_expectations.expectations as gxe
207	    from great_expectations.expectations.expectation import Expectation
208	
209	    from backend.app.datasources.gx_runner import _expectation_class_name
210	
211	    class_name = _expectation_class_name(expectation_type)
212	    expectation_cls = getattr(gxe, class_name, None)
213	    # The issubclass guard keeps a crafted type from resolving to a non-expectation
214	    # module attribute via the title-casing getattr.
215	    if expectation_cls is None or not (
216	        isinstance(expectation_cls, type) and issubclass(expectation_cls, Expectation)
217	    ):
218	        raise CheckConfigInvalidError(
219	            f"unknown expectation_type {expectation_type!r} — not a Great Expectations "
220	            "expectation",
221	            detail={"expectation_type": expectation_type},
222	        )
223	    try:
224	        expectation_cls(**config)
225	    except Exception as exc:
226	        # pydantic ValidationError (missing/wrong-typed/extra kwargs) or a GX
227	        # root-validator error; the message is user-actionable, so surface it.
228	        raise CheckConfigInvalidError(

input.new_string:
def test_create_accepts_long_but_legitimate_config_string(
    client: TestClient, db_session: Any
) -> None:
    # The cap must never reject what the runner would execute: a 1.5K regex is
    # valid GX and runs fine on the worker (the cap targets 100KB-class junk).
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks",
        json=_payload(
            expectation_type="expect_column_values_to_match_regex",
            config={"column": "sku", "regex": "(a|b)" * 250},
        ),
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201


def test_create_rejects_oversized_config_key_with_bounded_echo(
    client: TestClient, db_session: Any
) -> None:
    # Dict KEYS are capped too, and the 422 must not round-trip the oversized
    # input through the error envelope / logs.
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    huge_key = "K" * 200_000
    resp = client.post(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks", json=_payload(config={huge_key: {"x": "y"}})
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    assert len(resp.text) < 2_000


def test_update_name_only_allowed_on_legacy_invalid_config(
    client: TestClient, db_session: Any
) -> None:
    # A pre-#651 row whose stored config GX rejects must stay renamable /
    # threshold-tweakable — only a PATCH that touches the expectation itself
    # re-runs GX validation (there is no config backfill).
    sid = _suite_id(client, db_session)
    legacy = Check(
        suite_id=uuid.UUID(sid),
        name="legacy junk",
        expectation_type="expect_totally_made_up_thing",
        config={"colunm": "x" * 50_000},
    )
    db_session.add(legacy)
    db_session.commit()

    renamed = client.patch(f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks/{legacy.id}", json={"name": "renamed"})
    assert renamed.status_code == 200
    assert renamed.json()["name"] == "renamed"
    threshold = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks/{legacy.id}", json={"warn_threshold": 5}
    )
    assert threshold.status_code == 200
    # Touching the config still gates on GX — the junk can't be perpetuated.
    still_junk = client.patch(
        f"/api/v1/suites/{sid}/checks/{legacy.id}", json={"config": {"colunm": "x"}}
    )
    assert still_junk.status_code == 422

244	
245	def record_check_version(
246	    session: Session, check: Check, *, actor_id: uuid.UUID | None
247	) -> CheckVersion:
248	    """Append an immutable snapshot of `check`'s current state as its next
249	    version (a per-check sequence starting at 1). The caller commits — this only
250	    adds the row, so the snapshot and the create/update it records commit
251	    atomically. The `(check_id, version_no)` unique constraint is the backstop
252	    against a concurrent double-write computing the same number (rare under v1's
253	    single-tenant, low-concurrency editing).
254	
255	    `check.id` must be populated (flush or commit the check first).
256	    """
257	    # MAX over no rows is NULL → None; `or 0` makes the first version 1.
258	    current_max = session.scalar(
259	        select(func.max(CheckVersion.version_no)).where(CheckVersion.check_id == check.id)
260	    )
261	    next_no = (current_max or 0) + 1
262	    version = CheckVersion(
263	        check_id=check.id,
264	        version_no=next_no,
265	        name=check.name,
266	        kind=check.kind,
267	        expectation_type=check.expectation_type,
268	        config=check.config,
269	        warn_threshold=check.warn_threshold,

340	
341	
342	def get_check(session: Session, suite_id: uuid.UUID, check_id: uuid.UUID) -> Check:
343	    """Fetch a check, enforcing that it belongs to `suite_id` (else 404)."""
344	    check = session.get(Check, check_id)
345	    if check is None or check.suite_id != suite_id:
346	        raise CheckNotFoundError(
347	            "check not found",
348	            detail={"suite_id": str(suite_id), "check_id": str(check_id)},
349	        )
350	    return check
351	
352	
353	def update_check(
354	    session: Session,
355	    suite_id: uuid.UUID,
356	    check_id: uuid.UUID,
357	    *,
358	    name: str | None = None,
359	    expectation_type: str | None = None,
360	    config: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
361	    warn_threshold: Decimal | None = None,
362	    fail_threshold: Decimal | None = None,
363	    critical_threshold: Decimal | None = None,
364	    actor_id: uuid.UUID | None = None,
365	) -> Check:
366	    """Partial update, snapshotting the post-update state as a new version (#280).
367	
368	    Follows the codebase PATCH convention (connections / suites): a `None`
369	    argument means "not provided", so an omitted field is left unchanged. v1 has
370	    no clear-to-NULL path for thresholds; recreate the check to drop one.
371	    """
372	    check = get_check(session, suite_id, check_id)
373	    # Compute the effective post-patch values and validate them BEFORE touching
374	    # the ORM object: a rejected update must leave nothing dirty in the session
375	    # (mutate-then-raise would let a later commit on the same session persist
376	    # the invalid state). `kind` is immutable on update, so it's read off the
377	    # existing check.
378	    new_expectation_type = (
379	        expectation_type if expectation_type is not None else check.expectation_type
380	    )
381	    new_config = config if config is not None else check.config
382	    new_fail = fail_threshold if fail_threshold is not None else check.fail_threshold
383	    new_critical = (
384	        critical_threshold if critical_threshold is not None else check.critical_threshold
385	    )
386	    if check.kind in MONITOR_KINDS:
387	        suite = get_suite(session, suite_id)
388	        validate_monitor_check(
389	            check.kind,
390	            new_config,
391	            expectation_type=new_expectation_type,
392	            connection_type=_connection_type(session, suite),
393	            fail_threshold=new_fail,
394	            critical_threshold=new_critical,
395	        )
396	    elif is_custom_sql(new_expectation_type):
397	        suite = get_suite(session, suite_id)
398	        validate_custom_sql_check(
399	            expectation_type=new_expectation_type,
400	            config=new_config,
401	            connection_type=_connection_type(session, suite),
402	        )
403	    elif expectation_type is not None or config is not None:
404	        # GX-validate only when the PATCH touches the expectation itself: a
405	        # rename or threshold tweak must stay possible on a pre-#651 check whose
406	        # stored config today's pinned GX rejects (there is no config backfill —
407	        # such a row would otherwise be un-editable until delete-and-recreate).
408	        validate_expectation_check(new_expectation_type, new_config)
409	
410	    if name is not None:
411	        check.name = name
412	    if expectation_type is not None:
413	        check.expectation_type = expectation_type
414	    if config is not None:
415	        check.config = config
416	    if warn_threshold is not None:
417	        check.warn_threshold = warn_threshold
418	    if fail_threshold is not None:
419	        check.fail_threshold = fail_threshold
420	    if critical_threshold is not None:
421	        check.critical_threshold = critical_threshold
422	    # Only snapshot a real change: a no-op PATCH (empty body, or fields set to
423	    # their current values) must not mint a duplicate version — that would fill
424	    # the history drawer with noise and defeat "see previous config". SQLAlchemy
425	    # reports net changes, so setting a field to its existing value isn't dirty.
426	    if session.is_modified(check):
427	        record_check_version(session, check, actor_id=actor_id)
428	    try:
429	        session.commit()

"""Check endpoint tests against a real Postgres (db_session) via TestClient.

Checks are nested under a suite. A connection + suite are created per test for
the FK chain; auth runs in dev-bypass (conftest). Skips without
TEST_DATABASE_URL.
"""

import uuid
from collections.abc import Iterator
from datetime import UTC, datetime, timedelta
from typing import Any

import pytest
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from backend.app.core.auth import get_current_user
from backend.app.datasources.base import CheckOutcome, SuiteOutcome
from backend.app.db.models import Connection, Result, Run, Suite, User
from backend.app.db.session import get_db
from backend.app.main import app
from backend.app.services import dryrun_service


@pytest.fixture
def client(db_session: Any) -> Iterator[TestClient]:
    app.dependency_overrides[get_db] = lambda: db_session
    try:
        yield TestClient(app)
    finally:
        app.dependency_overrides.clear()


def _suite_id(client: TestClient, db_session: Any, conn_type: str = "snowflake") -> str:
    """Create a connection (ORM) + suite (API) and return the suite id.

    `conn_type` lets a test pick the datasource (e.g. 's3' to exercise custom-SQL
    datasource gating); defaults to Snowflake.
    """
    owner = User(aad_object_id=uuid.uuid4().hex, email="owner@example.com")
    db_session.add(owner)
    db_session.flush()
    config = {"account": "ab12345.eu-west-1"} if conn_type == "snowflake" else {}
    conn = Connection(
        name=f"{conn_type}-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}",
        type=conn_type,
        env="dev",
        config=config,
        created_by=owner.id,
    )
    db_session.add(conn)
    db_session.commit()
    resp = client.post(
        "/api/v1/suites",
        json={"name": "finance", "description": None, "connection_id": str(conn.id)},
    )
    return str(resp.json()["id"])
